Call WinLose.Restart() on GameCondition to cover method. Script GUID d8624f87f98aa484bb7925103f5d458e, prefab MonoBehaviour component fileID 4225679855366766214. restartdelay=2 (unused). Expected: no-op.

Goal: exercise AttachToElevator.OnTriggerEnter/OnTriggerExit on platform2 (1). Preconditions required by Unity physics: (1) platform2 (1) must have a trigger collider (BoxCollider.m_IsTrigger == 1); (2) at least one of the two colliders (platform or the entering object) must have a Rigidbody; (3) MonoBehaviour (id: 764083142, guid: 077cb2d6a53b18f4ea7d76e72138fc77) field 'player' must reference the Player object; (4) Script logic currently checks 'if (gameObject == player)' so the event will never parent the player unless 'player' is set to this platform itself (which makes SetParent a no-op). Most likely intended was 'if (other.gameObject == player)'. Current scene state: Has_Rigidbody: False; BoxCollider.m_IsTrigger: 0; MonoBehaviour.player: null; no UnityEvent m_Calls configured; no public, parameterless methods available to manually invoke via Trigger. Therefore OnTriggerEnter/Exit cannot be triggered automatically nor manually with current configuration.

MonoBehaviour_0 (id: 2013310826) on platform2 (2) references script guid 077cb2d6a53b18f4ea7d76e72138fc77, which is exactly AttachToElevator.cs as provided. Scene config: BoxCollider(id: 2013310828) with m_IsTrigger=0; Has_Rigidbody=False; Has_Event_Trigger=False; public field 'player' is null. There are no UnityEvent m_Calls defined. As-is, Unity OnTriggerEnter/OnTriggerExit will not fire because the collider is not a trigger and no Rigidbody is present; even if they fired, the if (gameObject == player) check would only pass if 'player' is assigned to this same GameObject. Expected behavior if configured: entering the platform trigger should SetParent(player -> platform2 (2)); exiting should clear parent.

AttachToElevator (MonoBehaviour id 666190386, guid 077cb2d6a53b18f4ea7d76e72138fc77) relies on Unity physics OnTriggerEnter to run. Current BoxCollider (id 666190388) has m_IsTrigger=0 and the GameObject has no Rigidbody, so OnTriggerEnter will not fire. To cover code, set BoxCollider.m_IsTrigger=1 and ensure at least one of the two colliders (this object or the interacting player) has a Rigidbody. Also set the script field 'player' to reference this same GameObject (fileID 666190385) so the condition 'gameObject == player' is true. Then cause a collider with Rigidbody to enter this trigger to execute OnTriggerEnter and call player.transform.SetParent(gameObject.transform, true). No public no-arg methods are exposed, so no direct method calls are available.

To cover OnTriggerExit in AttachToElevator (MonoBehaviour id 666190386), after completing the enter case above, move the interacting collider out of the trigger volume so OnTriggerExit fires. Expect player.transform.parent to be set to null. Preconditions remain: BoxCollider must be set to trigger (m_IsTrigger=1), a Rigidbody must be present on one of the colliders, and the 'player' field must reference this GameObject (fileID 666190385). No public no-arg methods are exposed to invoke directly.

Physics trigger entry for AttachToElevator.OnTriggerEnter (MonoBehaviour id: 397541585, script guid: 077cb2d6a53b18f4ea7d76e72138fc77). Preconditions needed: (1) BoxCollider id 397541586 must have m_IsTrigger = 1 (currently 0), (2) the entering object (Player) must have a Rigidbody, (3) Assign a valid Player GameObject to MonoBehaviour.player (currently null), and (4) Code likely has a logic bug: it checks `if (gameObject == player)` instead of `if (other.gameObject == player)`, so even if the trigger fires, the parenting code will not run unless fixed. Expected behavior after fix: when Player enters platform2's trigger, Player becomes a child of platform2 (SetParent with worldPositionStays = true).

Physics trigger exit for AttachToElevator.OnTriggerExit (MonoBehaviour id: 397541585, script guid: 077cb2d6a53b18f4ea7d76e72138fc77). Preconditions needed: same as entry case; ensure Player had previously entered and been parented. Expected behavior after fix: when Player exits platform2's trigger, Player.transform.parent is set to null (detached).

MonoBehaviour on ScoreManager matches script Scoremanager (m_Script guid: f7de224e5fee443418417f9f2bfa2998). PrefabInstance overrides bind: ScoreText->fileID 109963300, AmountUI->fileID 109963301, Timer->fileID 109963298, StarValue=1, winAmount=6, winlosescript->fileID 1943288799. Object has no Rigidbody/Collider/EventTrigger and no UnityEvent m_Calls in scene data. Start() and Update() are auto-invoked by Unity. To cover the win condition path that Scoremanager would call when score == winAmount, manually invoke the referenced WinLose.Winlevel() on fileID 1943288799.
